notes: Sub-band arrival-time (tilt) diagnostic for the CHIME-primary DM adoption: per-sub-band on-pulse arrival time vs frequency for phineas, isha (the two discrepant sightlines) and hamilton (control), each band dedispersed at both candidate DMs on a shared time ruler. CHIME cold-plasma expectations for the two DMs fan out by ~0.8-1.6 ms; DSA expectations collapse to a sub-sample stripe. Inputs: the 6 relevant ~/Data/Faber2026/dsa110/DSA_bursts/*_cntr_bpc.npy waterfalls and analysis/dm-joint-phase-v2/results/fits.json. ADDED 2026-07-15 (Faber2026 #83). Producer in the submodule pin: FLITS #185 merged (f9d7dfe) and is an ancestor of pin 17d9d266 (verified 2026-07-17). External raw data still prevent fresh-clone execution.

UPDATED 2026-07-17 (fig:toa-offset-decomposition, sec:toa): per-burst decomposition of the canonical CHIME-DSA 400-MHz arrival offset into the DRAO--OVRO geometric pedestal plus residual, with a secondary equivalent-shared-DM axis (d(offset)/dDM=-9.4078 ms per pc cm^-3). Reads pipeline/crossmatching/toa_crossmatch_results.json (measured_offset_ms, peak_measured_offset_ms, model_correction_status, geometric_delay_ms); deterministic, no external data. At the pinned FLITS state all twelve model corrections are diagnostic_only:MARGINAL and unreviewed, so measured_offset_ms fails closed to the observed-peak value; the producer enforces that invariant and does not consume model_corrected_offset_ms directly. tests/test_toa_offset_decomposition.py and tests/test_consistency_audit.py guard the field contract; pipeline/tests/test_association.py exercises the same peak residual pedestal.
